$comment: The `gap` package — https://pub.dev/packages/gap. The second catalog this repository has, and the first for a package rather than a framework. ADR-18 designed for exactly this: 'adding a package means adding a line in AdapterRegistry.production() and a file beside it — and nothing else, anywhere.' M4-I is where that claim was first tested. `Gap` is the single most-used unsupported widget in the M0 corpus at 115 instantiations — more than `Container`, more than `SizedBox` — and it is not a framework widget at all.

widgets.$comment: `Gap(16)` — a fixed extent along the parent's main axis. Its one argument is positional, which is why it reached UIR as `_positional0` before this entry existed (ADR-0023). The name is the SDK's own field name.
widgets.name: Gap

widgets.$comment: `SliverGap` is the sliver form. Present so its structure survives extraction and the generator refuses it by name with the sliver reason rather than as an unknown widget.
widgets.name: SliverGap

widgets.$comment: `MaxGap(16)` — a gap that yields when space is short. Its `maxExtent` is the *most* it takes, which is a flex behaviour rather than a fixed size.
widgets.name: MaxGap
